hitchens_question: When did we decide that the phrase "battle-ready laser weapons" should sound as mundane as a quarterly earnings report, while simultaneously pretending that villages don't simply vanish from maps without someone in an air-conditioned office signing the requisite paperwork? The same week we learn that banned Russian submunitions are falling on Mali and "demolished" Lebanese towns are visible from space, we're told the real concern is hackers posting mean things about America on a Space Force official's Instagram account. Is our capacity for moral proportion so thoroughly cauterized that we now measure atrocity by its potential to trend on social media rather than by the number of people it kills?
